content.problemStatement: Print the number 108 using arithmetic operations on the numbers 9 and 12.
content.outputFormat: Print the integer 108 followed by a newline.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The program multiplies 9 by 12, yielding 108, which is printed.
Even if the judge runs multiple hidden test files, each file contains no input; the program always prints 108.
The internal loop runs exactly once, so the output remains a single line with 108.

solution.approach: The required value can be obtained by a single arithmetic operation: multiply the two given numbers 9 and 12. Since the problem does not provide any input, we simply compute 9*12 and print the result. To follow the platform convention of handling multiple test cases, we internally set T = 1 and repeat the computation once.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) – a constant amount of work is performed.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
